author: @user-dr2yz8um3d
text:
Low and behold, the movie is 1 year old!!!

My most hyped up movie of the fall
Not as bad as many are making it
Violent but funny, exciting, opens more of the DCEU
Dwayne Johnson has a lot of charisma and Sarah Shahi and especially Pierce Brosnan as Dr. Fate
The team called the Justice Society of America is fun even if not all the members are fleshed out
Plus the action scenes are amazing and the plot is easy to follow despite a lot of slo mo and exposition next to the flashbacks
Great cameo too from a familiar actor from a previous film
A very weak villain though as you'd expect
Says a lot about having a champion in a 3rd world country as far as representation, diplomacy, heroism, choosing to be a destroyer or savior, being worthy of god like powers or not, being up to fate to decide our outcome
15 years of Johnson playing this character was worth the wait, this is one of my favorite DC comic book characters now

author: @sudilos1172
text:
The writing on this is SOOOOOOOO Terrible! The opening UGH! "The worlds first kingdom." and it's first ARMY? Why would it even need an Army? And they of course yes. Metal weapons, swords. Not clubs, or spears. The evil King stole the kingdom. But no he needs a crown. And then he enslaves all of his people. But he forces them to mine. But they've never found any of the material to know that they won't actually get a reward. Urghhh. Also idk if it's my side monitor or not, but the frame rate when the scenes pan are dizzying. Other movies don't do that thou. Shrugs.
Update: Watched the whole thing on my main tv now. I really loved it! Just remember, the whole anti-hero dark hero dark knight bs is just another way to get you to accept evil/darkness/bad guys in the role of your Heros. They're bad not because they're badass. They're bad because like in real life. They'd just let you be crushed by a stone, and not actually move to save you at the last second.

author: @DrPhoenixJKZ
text:
Actually, this is one of my favourite superhero movies if not the most.
Number 1, Rock is one action I love.
Number 2, this movie is kinda similar to Batman, my favourite DC superhero. I know Batman do not kill, but Black Adam's story is just about as dark as Batman's. Shamefully, for many countries in poverty, and even developing countries, it have a lot of problems pictured here. Some of these countries are controlled by warlords and gangs, and some of them get environmental destruction problems due to big companies from developed countries. Quite unfair, but because of such problems are difficult to solve, and more importantly, have related benefits for companies in more developed countries, they get ignored quite a lot.
Example 1, rainforest destruction in South America and Indonesia largely due to big corporations and companies in the United States. Palm oil industry, which include forcing indigenous people to lose their lands and do excess labour. Related news can be found on Rainforest Action Network websites.
Example 2, recently, a lot of people in China meet con artists from Thailand and Burma, lose ten billions of RMB per year. And many Chinese people even got kidnapped through this way for ransom and organ trades. But, you know what? Such trades could only happen because of there are a lot of Chinese members among such con artist groups! How painful is that! And for those drug dealers, conartists groups, military gangs, they are too powerful for local government to control. Not only that, because they have both local power and Chinese people in their teams, they have people work for them among local police force, and very likely also inside Chinese governments.

I study biology, and that includes a bit about environment and society, so I know the world pictured here is very true. And for people in those countries, they really do need heroes like Black Adam here to free them.
